items.title: SA Premier Peter Malinauskas apologises for failing to deliver on his promise to 'fix the ramping crisis'
items.summary: South Australian Premier Peter Malinauskas says there is "no shirking the fact" his government has failed to deliver on its key election promise to fix the ramping crisis — a failure for which he has apologised.

items.title: How Donald Trump and JD Vance are 'philosophically a little bit different' on Iran
items.summary: From MAGA-loathing "hillbilly" to vice-president, JD Vance's rise and rebirth have been something to behold. But the war in Iran may test his considerable political dexterity.

items.title: Stakeholders race to find Liberty Bell Bay smelter buyer as job losses loom
items.summary: A northern Tasmanian business group wants to secure a buyer for Australia's only manganese alloy smelter as the corporate regulator threatens its current owners with liquidation.

items.title: Fears grow for Iranian footballers as activists mount last-ditch rescue bid from Malaysia
items.summary: Iranian activists are racing against time to convince members of the country's female football team to turn back from Malaysia, as fears grow that the women are being coerced home by regime officials.

items.title: Putin and Trump gave 'old, fat, lazy' Europe wake-up call, Estonian foreign minister says
items.summary: Estonia's foreign minister declares that Europe was like an "old, fat, lazy cat" in the aftermath of the Cold War and has vowed that NATO forces will strike "into the heart of Russia" if Moscow invades Baltic countries.

items.title: Iranian women's team not the first athletes to seek asylum while competing overseas
items.summary: The choice for some Iranian women's soccer team members to stay in Australia is not the first time athletes have travelled for a sporting competition and chosen to stay.

items.title: Dennis Richardson's exit puts antisemitism royal commissioner under more pressure
items.summary: As soon as the royal commission was announced, Dennis Richardson must have known his situation was going to be tricky.

items.title: MP whose electorate gets largest capital gains tax discount wants to rein it in
items.summary: New analysis shows five of the six electorates with the largest capital gains tax discount benefits are held by independents.

items.title: On the Iraq-Iran border Kurdish troops are gathering to fight their own battle against the Khamenei regime
items.summary: On Iraq's border with Iran, troops are amassing as Iranian Kurds from across the globe gather to revolt after 47 years of being the victims of an oppressive and cruel regime.

items.title: What should you do with the family home after a divorce?
items.summary: For many Australians, the decision to divorce has become even more complicated because of the rising cost of housing. Some are now calling for policy changes to make it easier for single parents to re-enter the housing market.

items.title: Pokémon's virtual citizen science taught Australian entomology how to catch 'em all
items.summary: As Pokémon turns 30, a video game inspired by insect-collecting continues to shape how scientists think and teach about bugs today.

items.title: Hugo Weaving talks about loving art, hating learning lines, and what's next
items.summary: Hugo Weaving may be an internationally renowned actor of both stage and screen, but he says he never feels ready for his next big role.

items.title: Weekly news quiz: A new Nationals leader, a curious crocodile and the duelling Perrys
items.summary: Think you've been paying attention to the news cycle this week? Test your memory here.

items.title: Women's Asian Cup 2026: The full-throttle, mysterious North Korean side looking to rain on the Matildas' parade
items.summary: Sensational protests, head-scratching substitutions and some seriously impressive play — North Korea is one of the Asian Cup's best sides, and certainly its most mysterious. Here is what the Matildas can expect in their blockbuster quarterfinal tonight in Perth.

items.title: Attacks on veteran MP force Victorian Liberal party to reissue campaign material
items.summary: Liberal party MPs and insiders have been infuriated by the publishing of a preselection candidate's blistering statement about a veteran MP.

items.title: Israel's military drops prosecution of reservists who allegedly raped Palestinian detainee
items.summary: Israel's military drops the prosecution of five reservists accused of the violent rape of a Palestinian man at the Sde Teiman prison, under the cover of the war with Iran.

items.title: Oil tankers burn as Iran's new leader vows to keep Strait of Hormuz closed
items.summary: Iran's new supreme leader says the Strait of Hormuz should remain closed as a tool of pressure, vowing to "obtain compensation from the enemy".

items.title: ADF member facing 'significant' assault charges against colleague granted bail
items.summary: A Darwin-based soldier accused of assaulting and robbing a fellow service person has been bailed to travel home to NSW.

items.title: Drought-hit WA pastoralists plead for funds to move hay stored 1,000km away
items.summary: After years of drought, pastoralists in WA are desperate for fodder for their cattle. A charity has a supply in storage for them — but no money for fuel to transport it.

items.title: Queensland coroner to probe whether death of eight-year-old Elizabeth Struhs was preventable
items.summary: The Queensland coroner will examine interactions the Struhs family had with government agencies before the eight-year-old's death at the family's Toowoomba home in 2022.

items.title: 'Absolute madness': Broncos remain winless after Eels win bizarre NRL clash
items.summary: The Broncos' defence is labelled "pathetic" as they surrender a 20-6 lead, hit back in the second half but eventually lose 40-32 on the back of future teammate Jonah Pezet's game-sealing try.

items.summary: News from the world of finance and business. Top stories in text, video and audio. Business video news bulletin. Features and analysis.

items.title: Hobart Clinic chief resigns months after appointment amid reported clash with board
items.summary: The Hobart Clinic's chief executive has resigned as the state government commissions a review of the mental health hospital's corporate governance.

items.title: Blues hang on for scratchy but vital win over Tigers at the MCG
items.summary: Carlton kicks only one goal in the second half, but it is just enough to hold off a late charge by a wayward Richmond at the MCG.

items.title: SAPOL powers to search for weapons extended to four regional shopping centres
items.summary: Police have named the first regional Declared Shopping Centres, which grant police additional move-on and weapon search powers. The laws already apply to several Adelaide shopping precincts and have led to multiple weapon seizures.

items.title: Parents and disability advocates shocked as specialist service AEIOU closes with no notice
items.summary: In a statement published on Wednesday, early intervention care provider AEIOU announced the board had appointed external administrators to the organisation.

items.title: Man who killed Perth music promoter Andrew Scally in suburban street sentenced to jail
items.summary: Jardyn Rigo is sentenced to more than six years in jail for killing music promoter Andrew Scally in a street fight.

items.title: Disaster assistance payments have been expanded in the Northern Territory. Here's what you need to know
items.summary: Residents in more flood-affected communities across the Top End are now eligible for hardship payments, with disaster assistance also extended further in the Barkly and Central Australia.

items.summary: World number three Novak Djokovic falls to defending champion Jack Draper in a pulsating fourth-round match at the prestigious Indian Wells tournament.

items.title: Albanese government won't say the 'w' word about Iran

items.summary: Heamasi Makasini has shown off his talent in both rugby codes, but Wests Tigers are hopeful they will keep the 18-year-old at the club once his contract expires.

items.summary: The oil price has surged again amid Iranian strikes on oil tankers and the fear there could be more attacks to come.

items.title: SA Liberal candidate no longer running for party after podcast comments

items.summary: The boil water alert for Darwin has been lifted after testing confirmed the region's water was free of E.coli and other harmful bacteria.

items.title: Deeper oil crisis could add full percentage point to inflation, Treasury warns
items.summary: Treasury has advised the government that if the oil price averages $US100 per barrel for three months, there would be a temporary 0.5 percentage point spike to headline inflation.

items.title: Atlassian cuts 1,600 jobs from global workforce as AI slashes labour needs
items.summary: Atlassian's Australian workforce will account for 30 per cent of the tech giant's global job cuts, with the freed-up funds to be invested in AI and enterprise sales.

items.title: Country councils putting compulsory voting for WA's local government elections back on agenda

items.title: Mother jailed for criminal neglect of three children, including 6yo Charlie, who died

items.title: Beth Mooney sold for record fee in Women's Hundred auction

items.summary: Australian sports presenter Mel McLaughlin says she has had treatment for lung cancer, after being absent from TV screens since January.

items.title: NSW defeat Tasmania by four wickets to win One Day Cup final
items.summary: A steady hand from skipper Kurtis Patterson, after early damage by Sean Abbott and Ben Dwarshuis, has helped NSW to a One Day Cup title with a rain-hit four-wicket win over Tasmania.

items.title: Islamic Society criticises 'competition' to attack Muslims as SA Liberals stand by candidate
items.summary: An organisation representing South Australian Muslims criticises the Liberal Party and One Nation for what it says is a rush to attack Islam "more harshly in order to gain political support" ahead of the state election.

items.title: After watching all 211 of Alex Johnston's NRL tries, these five are the most important

items.title: Former ASIO boss Dennis Richardson says he was 'surplus to requirements' of royal commission
items.summary: Former ASIO boss Dennis Richardson says he resigned from the royal commission into antisemitism because he felt he was overpaid and "surplus to requirements".

items.title: WA pensioner Bill Turner chooses to 'live for today' as state's rental crisis hits hard
items.summary: At 76 and stuck on the waitlist for public housing, Bill Turner is on the brink of homelessness, unable to afford his own rental in a housing market at breaking point — a situation that's left him feeling "deserted".

items.title: Australia's summers are getting longer with more extreme heat
items.summary: Australia has always been hot. On average, summer is now one degree warmer than it was 50 years ago. But that number hides the stark reality that the summers our kids experience today are much more extreme and longer than they used to be.

items.summary: Respected VFL commentator Jason Bennett returns to the coverage in 2026 after the ABC picked up the broadcast rights to the league.
